description: §6.13.1 debounce TRAILING commit: writes to a `<val debounced=1s>` cell coalesce, and after the window expires the LATEST held value commits to the cell. Three rapid clicks drive a plain `<clicks>` counter 0->3 (immediate) and each sets `@val = @clicks` (debounced). With NO time advanced, @val stays 0 — every write is coalesced and NONE has committed yet (the control observation). A single `advance-time` past the 1s window fires the trailing commit, landing the latest value (3). Regression guard for g-debounce-throttle-trailing-no-commit (S236): before the fix the expiry closure re-routed back into the timing wrapper and re-armed forever, so @val never left 0 even after advance-time.
rationale: §6.13.1 debounce: each write arms a timer for ms; a new write within the window cancels+re-arms; on expiry the coalesced value commits through the reactive write path (subscribers + derived + DOM). The commit MUST route past the timing wrapper (bypass) so it lands in the cell rather than re-arming. Under the deterministic virtual clock the three same-tick writes all fall inside the 1s window (coalesced), so the trailing fire is the ONLY commit — driven exclusively by advance-time.
